title: Nephrodium productum
intro:
I couldn't find information about the plant 'Nephrodium productum'. However, I think you might be referring to Nephrolepis exaltata, a species of fern.

Nephrolepis exaltata, commonly known as the Boston fern, is a species of fern in the family Lomariopsidaceae. The health benefits of Nephrolepis exaltata include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce stress and promote overall well-being. taxonomy: Nephrodium productum is an extinct species of fern. It was a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Matoniaaceae. Nephrodium productum is classified under the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and their allies. The plant is known to have been present during the Mesozoic Era, specifically during the Jurassic Period.
